old_value: {"id":"personal-password-manager-g6v","title":"Address npm audit vulnerabilities in Nx dependencies","description":"5 high severity vulnerabilities exist in minimatch via Nx 22.5.1. Before modifying any package versions manually, use 'nx migrate' first to let Nx manage its own version upgrades and migration scripts correctly. Only fall back to manual version edits if nx migrate cannot resolve the issue.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"chore","owner":"travis@modernmachine.io","created_at":"2026-03-13T01:25:00Z","created_by":"Travis Daudelin","updated_at":"2026-03-13T01:25:00Z"}

new_value: Ran nx migrate latest — bumped Nx packages from 22.5.1 to 22.5.4, resolving all minimatch high severity vulns. Remaining 21 vulns (3 low, 10 moderate, 8 high) are in transitive dev dependencies (jsdom, @swc/cli, koa via @nx/module-federation) that require breaking changes to fix and do not ship in the extension bundle.
